back_translation: A song. A Psalm of Asaph.
O God, do not be silent; do not be speechless; do not be still, O God.
See how Your enemies rage, and how those who hate You have raised their heads.
With cunning they scheme against Your people and conspire against those You cherish,
saying, 'Come, let us wipe them out as a nation, so that the name of Israel is remembered no more.'
For with one mind they conspire together; they form an alliance against You—
the tents of Edom and the Ishmaelites, of Moab and the Hagrites,
of Gebal, Ammon, and Amalek, of Philistia with the people of Tyre.
Even Assyria has joined them, lending strength to the descendants of Lot. Selah
Do to them as You did to Midian, as to Sisera and Jabin at the River Kishon,
who perished at Endor and became like dung on the ground.
Make their nobles like Oreb and Zeeb, and all their princes like Zebah and Zalmunna,
who said, 'Let us seize the pastures of God for ourselves.'
O my God, make them like whirling tumbleweed, like chaff before the wind.
As fire consumes a forest, and as a flame sets the mountains ablaze,
so pursue them with Your tempest and terrify them with Your storm.
Cover their faces with shame, so that they may seek Your name, O LORD.
May they be ashamed and terrified forever; may they perish in disgrace.
So that they may know that You, whose name is the LORD, are alone the Most High over all the earth.
